description_wiki: Japan is an island country in East Asia.

description: country located primarily in North America
description_wiki: The United States of America (USA), also known as the United States (U.S.) or America, is a country primarily located in North America.

surface_forms: the beginning of World War II
Japan was defeated in the World War II
description_wiki: World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

surface_forms: The Japanese attack on Pearl Harbor
description: 1941 Japanese surprise attack on the U.S
description_wiki: On December 7, 1941, the Empire of Japan launched a surprise military strike on the United States Pacific Fleet at its naval base at Pearl Harbor on Oahu, Hawaii.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: California () is a state in the Western United States that lies on the Pacific Coast.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Utah is a landlocked state in the Mountain West subregion of the  Western United States.

aliases: The town of Our Lady the Queen of the Angels of the Little Portion
description: seat of Los Angeles County, and largest city in California, United States
description_wiki: Los Angeles (often referred to by its initials, LA) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of California, and the commercial, financial, and cultural center of Southern California.

description: public coeducational space-grant research university in Salt Lake City, Utah, United States
description_wiki: The University of Utah (the U, U of U, or simply Utah) is a public research university in Salt Lake City, Utah, United States.

aliases: University of California at Los Angeles
State Normal School at Los Angeles
description: public university in Los Angeles, California, USA
description_wiki: The University of California, Los Angeles (UCLA) is a public land-grant research university in the Westwood neighborhood of Los Angeles, California, United States.

Nisei (二世, "second generation") is a Japanese-language term used in countries in North America and South America to specify the ethnically Japanese children born in the new country to Japanese-born immigrants, or Issei.

description: Japanese people who were the first generation of immigrants from Japan
description_wiki: Issei (一世, "first generation") are Japanese immigrants to countries in North America and South America.

description: city in and county seat of King County, State of Washington, United States
description_wiki: Seattle (  see-AT-əl) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Washington and the Pacific Northwest region of North America.
The Great Depression was a severe global economic downturn from 1929 to 1939.

description: a polytheistic and animistic religion originating in Japan
description_wiki: Shinto (神道, Shintō; Japanese pronunciation: [ɕiꜜn.toː]), also called Shintoism, is the native religion and former ethnic cult of Japan.

description: Abrahamic monotheistic religion founded on the teachings, person and life of Jesus
description_wiki: Christianity is an Abrahamic monotheistic religion, which states that Jesus is the Son of God and rose from the dead after his crucifixion, whose coming as the messiah (Christ) was prophesied in the Old Testament and chronicled in the New Testament.

description: city in Utah County, Utah, United States
description_wiki: Springville is a city in Utah County, Utah, United States, that is part of the Provo–Orem metropolitan area.

surface_forms: the present address in Salt Lake City
description: city in and county seat of Salt Lake County, Utah, United States and the capital of Utah
description_wiki: Salt Lake City, often shortened to Salt Lake or SLC, is the capital and most populous city of the U.S. state of Utah.
